sn47.33:1.1: “Monks, those in whom the four establishings of mindfulness are neglected, in them the noble eightfold1 path leading to the right ending of suffering & stress is neglected.
sn47.33:1.2: Those in whom the four establishings of mindfulness are aroused, in them the noble eightfold path leading to the right ending of suffering & stress is aroused.
sn47.33:2.2: There is the case where a monk remains focused on the body in & of itself—ardent, alert, & mindful—subduing greed & distress with reference to the world.
sn47.33:2.3: He remains focused on feelings in & of themselves…
sn47.33:2.5: mental qualities in & of themselves—ardent, alert, & mindful—subduing greed & distress with reference to the world.
sn47.33:2.6: “Those in whom these four establishings of mindfulness are neglected, in them the noble eigthfold path leading to the right ending of suffering & stress is neglected.
sn47.33:2.7: Those in whom these four establishings of mindfulness are aroused, in them the noble eightfold path leading to the right ending of suffering & stress is aroused.”
